title: いつまで～のやら
titleEn: Expresses uncertainty regarding duration; dont know when... will end
structure: いつまで + Verb (dictionary form) + のやら
structureEn: いつまで + Verb (dictionary form) + のやら
explanation: Dùng để bày tỏ không biết tình trạng sẽ kéo dài đến bao giờ. Sắc thái lo lắng, chán nản hoặc bất lực. Cấu trúc: いつまで + Verb (dictionary form) + のやら.
explanationEn: The いつまで～のやら grammar point is used to express uncertainty about when something will end or finish. It is often used in situations where the speaker is unsure about when a particular state of affairs or event will conclude
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～が Verb られる
titleEn: Expresses potential or ability to do something; can, able to
structure: ❶ (Group 1) Verb: 書く → 書ける / 飲む → 飲める, etc. ❷ (Group 2) Verb-ます stem + られる (食べる→食べられる、見る→見られる)
structureEn: ❶ (Group 1) Verb: 書く → 書ける / 飲む → 飲める, etc. ❷ (Group 2) Verb-ます stem + られる (食べる→食べられる、見る→見られる)
explanation: Dùng để diễn tả khả năng hoặc năng lực làm một việc. Với động từ nhóm 1 đổi sang dạng khả năng, với nhóm 2 thường thêm られる. Cấu trúc: ❶ (Group 1) Verb: 書く → 書ける / 飲む → 飲める, etc. ❷ (Group 2) Verb-ます stem + られる (食べる→食べられる、見る→見られる).
explanationEn: The ～が Verb られる grammar pattern generally refers to the potential form of Japanese verbs (i.e., can do something). However, the exact formation depends on the verb group. For most Group 1 (五段) verbs, potential form changes the final -u to -eru (e.g., 書く→書ける). For Group 2 (一段) verbs, you often see ～られる (e.g., 食べる→食べられる). In English, it translates as can or able to
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～かと思いきや
titleEn: Expressing unexpected outcomes; just when I thought..., but
structure: Sentence + かと思いきや (Verb plain form, i-/na-Adjective, or Noun + だ + かと思いきや)
structureEn: Sentence + かと思いきや (Verb plain form, i-/na-Adjective, or Noun + だ + かと思いきや)
explanation: Dùng khi tưởng rằng X sẽ xảy ra, nhưng kết quả lại khác hẳn hoặc bất ngờ. Có thể hiểu là “cứ tưởng là..., nào ngờ...”. Cấu trúc: Sentence + かと思いきや (Verb plain form, i-/na-Adjective, or Noun + だ + かと思いきや).
explanationEn: The ～かと思いきや grammar point is used when the outcome turns out different from what was initially assumed or expected. It can be translated as just when I thought... or simply but (actually).... It often highlights that the speaker’s assumption was proven wrong by subsequent events
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～がゆえに
titleEn: Expresses the reason or cause; because, due to, as a result of
structure: Noun / Verb / Adjective + がゆえに
structureEn: Noun / Verb / Adjective + がゆえに
explanation: Dùng để nêu nguyên nhân hoặc lý do theo sắc thái trang trọng: “vì”, “do”. Thường xuất hiện trong văn viết hoặc lập luận. Cấu trúc: Noun / Verb / Adjective + がゆえに.
explanationEn: The ～がゆえに grammar point is used to express a reason or cause, often in more formal writing or speech. It roughly corresponds to because (of), due to, or as a result of in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～がゆえの Noun
titleEn: Expresses the reason or cause for something; because of, due to
structure: Noun / Verb-dictionary form + がゆえの + Noun
structureEn: Noun / Verb-dictionary form + がゆえの + Noun
explanation: Dùng để bổ nghĩa cho danh từ theo nghĩa “do/vì... mà có”. Mẫu này trang trọng và thường nêu nguyên nhân của một kết quả. Cấu trúc: Noun / Verb-dictionary form + がゆえの + Noun.
explanationEn: The ～がゆえの Noun pattern is used to describe a noun that exists or occurs due to some cause. It is often used in more literary or formal contexts
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～から Noun に 至る まで
titleEn: Expressing range from one thing to another; from...to, ranging from...to
structure: Noun (start) + から + Noun (end) + に至るまで
structureEn: Noun (start) + から + Noun (end) + に至るまで
explanation: Dùng để nêu phạm vi rất rộng từ điểm bắt đầu đến điểm kết thúc. Có thể hiểu là “từ... cho đến...”. Cấu trúc: Noun (start) + から + Noun (end) + に至るまで.
explanationEn: The ～から〜に至るまで grammar point emphasizes a range or scope extending from one extreme to another. It can be translated as from...to... or ranging from...to... in English. It can apply to people, places, times, objects, and so on
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～ごとく
titleEn: To seem like; as if; as though
structure: Verb-て + ごとく / Noun + の + ごとく
structureEn: Verb-て + ごとく / Noun + の + ごとく
explanation: Dùng để so sánh theo nghĩa “như”, “tựa như”, mang sắc thái văn chương hoặc trang trọng. Cấu trúc: Verb-て + ごとく / Noun + の + ごとく.
explanationEn: The ～ごとく grammar point is used to express similarity or resemblance. It is quite formal/literary, meaning as if, as though, or like. It can be used with verbs and nouns
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～こととて
titleEn: Used to express the reason or premise for a decision or judgment; since, because
structure: Verb (plain form) + こととて / Noun + の + こととて / (な-Adjective + な) + こととて
structureEn: Verb (plain form) + こととて / Noun + の + こととて / (な-Adjective + な) + こととて
explanation: Dùng để nêu lý do hoặc tiền đề cho một nhận định, thường trong lối nói trang trọng hoặc khiêm nhường. Có thể hiểu là “vì là... nên”. Cấu trúc: Verb (plain form) + こととて / Noun + の + こととて / (な-Adjective + な) + こととて.
explanationEn: The ～こととて grammar point expresses a reason or premise in a slightly formal or literary way. It can often be interpreted as since or because
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～ずにすんだ
titleEn: Expresses the idea of was able to do without; managed to avoid
structure: Verb-ず form + に + すんだ
structureEn: Verb-ず form + に + すんだ
explanation: Dùng để nói may mắn tránh được việc phải làm X hoặc không cần làm X. Có thể hiểu là “đã không phải...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-ず form + に + すんだ.
explanationEn: The ～ずにすんだ grammar point indicates that one was able to avoid doing something or managed to get by without doing it. It translates as managed to avoid or got by without
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: ～だろうとなかろうと
titleEn: Expresses regardless of whether it is or isnt
structure: Noun / i-Adjective / na-Adjective / Verb + だろうとなかろうと
structureEn: Noun / i-Adjective / na-Adjective / Verb + だろうとなかろうと
explanation: Dùng để nói dù có là X hay không phải X thì điều phía sau vẫn không thay đổi. Sắc thái trang trọng. Cấu trúc: Noun / i-Adjective / na-Adjective / Verb + だろうとなかろうと.
explanationEn: The ～だろうとなかろうと grammar point is used to express the idea of regardless of whether it is or isnt. It translates roughly as whether it is or not in English. This construction emphasizes that the speaker’s stance or action does not change, regardless of the two possible conditions
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
